evidence: ASan-linked real-library binary crashes before main during protobuf descriptor initialization (see validation/asan_output.txt), so it does not validate ParseAny directly. The same fresh PoC against the existing plain compiled libprotobuf.a demonstrates the bug on the real library: a 50,331,694-byte Any JSON payload parses successfully and increases resident memory by 146,194,432 bytes, showing ParseAny buffers and reparses oversized input without a size guard (see validation/plain_output.txt and validation/VALIDATION_STATUS.md).
